The Tang Dynasty (618–907 AD) represents a golden age in Chinese history, characterized by political stability, economic prosperity, and cultural flourishing. During this period, the empire expanded its influence across Central Asia, facilitated the Silk Road trade, and saw significant advancements in art, literature, and technology. The capital, Chang'an, became one of the largest and most cosmopolitan cities in the world.
